highlight.name: ab21b893-6e7a-b6a0-157a-dcf1e0f73ff5
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The design of the old application, if there was one, had accreted as one capability after another had been laid on top of the existing code, without any noticeable generalization or abstraction.

highlight.name: 81b7aeeb-617b-567c-2897-417993d0b846
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: But the second time around, if the developers perceive analysis to be a separate process, modeling happens in a less disciplined way.

highlight.name: b5983ea2-4420-b1a9-4612-7773869169bf
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The domain model is a set of concepts. The “domain layer” is the manifestation of that model and all directly related design elements. The design and implementation of business logic constitute the domain layer. In a MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN, the software constructs of the domain layer mirror the model concepts.

squiggly.name: a9edad7f-8cd0-cb32-026f-b757eaf22102
squiggly.title: Guest
squiggly.subject: 曲线
squiggly.contents: To compromise in implementation without losing the punch of a MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN requires a reframing of the basics.

squiggly.name: 75a162b1-bcc7-1e56-58ae-84576914848e
squiggly.title: Guest
squiggly.subject: 曲线
squiggly.contents: This discussion will start with the issues of designing and streamlining associations.

squiggly.name: c0722bca-2924-4d56-2e8e-117165e90f2b
squiggly.title: Guest
squiggly.subject: 曲线
squiggly.contents: Associations illustrate how crucial detailed implementation decisions are to the viability of a MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN.

squiggly.name: 23ca896b-aa5a-9cb0-5c7d-2d8aa83d03e7
squiggly.title: Guest
squiggly.subject: 曲线
squiggly.contents: Turning to the objects themselves, but continuing to scrutinize the relationship between detailed model choices and implementation concerns, we’ll focus on making distinctions among the three patterns of model elements that express the model: ENTITIES, VALUE OBJECTS, and SERVICES.

squiggly.name: 4ae6eb19-7e87-75a4-cafc-979e7f0f072d
squiggly.title: Guest
squiggly.subject: 曲线
squiggly.contents: Defining objects that capture concepts of the domain seems very intuitive on the surface, but serious challenges are lurking in the shades of meaning. Certain distinctions have emerged that clarify the meaning of model elements and tie into a body of design practices for carving out specific kinds of objects.

squiggly.name: 81eed04b-f2cd-8390-4bbf-f9c5ee9ea0a1
squiggly.title: Guest
squiggly.subject: 曲线
squiggly.contents: A SERVICE is something that is done for a client on request. In the technical layers of the software, there are many SERVICES. They emerge in the domain also, when some activity is modeled that corresponds to something the software must do, but does not correspond with state.

underline.name: 9ef6502b-3990-c0c8-f3e1-ff04dbcfe589
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: These ideas are conventional, and the modeling and design biases that follow from them have been written about before.

highlight.name: 4f4fb366-b81a-0b67-cd0d-9f0ddfd83f50
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Very often, deeper understanding leads to a “qualified” relationship.

highlight.name: 8784ad61-79a1-358b-5c27-31914a1fbe9b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Of course, the ultimate simplification is to eliminate an association altogether, if it is not essential to the job at hand or the fundamental meaning of the model objects.

highlight.name: 3cdc4b0f-7bac-d581-bbd9-163c1dba1711
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: This wouldn’t be true of all business situations (for example, if the lots need to be tracked), but whatever the particular rules, as constraints on associations are discovered they should be included in the model and implementation.

underline.name: 0b6a171a-5f1b-d84c-72cc-a7809517941c
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: This says there can be only one investment per stock.

highlight.name: e098922e-ab12-89a7-4e81-40a04bfbfd1d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Carefully distilling and constraining the model’s associations will take you a long way toward a MODEL- DRIVEN DESIGN. Now let’s turn to the objects themselves. Certain distinctions clarify the model while making for a more practical implementation. . . .

underline.name: 1a71058e-ac32-3378-d07b-ea4532b17984
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: Many objects are not fundamentally defined by their attributes, but rather by a thread of continuity and identity.

underline.name: 9c72a15b-d690-9802-4352-790927687a9d
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: The papers I was served described an apartment with holes in the walls, stains on the carpet, and a noxious liquid in the sink that gave off caustic fumes that had made the kitchen wallpaper peel.

highlight.name: 63eba4a4-d0d4-ea22-a6a4-bb4d3ece2fa9
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: plaintiff

highlight.name: a2c72fcf-a9c8-9d9e-ec86-4728acec26ac
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: tenant

highlight.name: 2d6a5579-e465-2720-385b-27f54aeea6d6
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: salvation.

underline.name: 76bfc506-af76-44da-4d36-9903dfe4f70f
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: In a software system for tracking accounts due, that modest “customer” object may have a more colorful side. It accumulates status by prompt payment or is turned over to a bill-collection agency for failure to pay. It may lead a double life in another system altogether when the sales force extracts customer data into its contact management software. In any case, it is unceremoniously squashed flat to be stored in a database table. When new business stops flowing from that source, the customer object will be retired to an archive, a shadow of its former self.

highlight.name: cd4c6a9f-c0a3-2316-e5ac-f6b04e4d62ec
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A conceptual identity has to be matched between multiple implementations of the objects, its stored forms, and real-world actors such as the phone caller. Attributes may not match. A sales representative may have entered an address update into the contact software, which is just being propagated to accounts due. Two customer contacts may have the same name. In distributed software, multiple users could be entering data from different sources, causing update transactions to propagate through the system to be reconciled in different databases asynchronously.

highlight.name: 1a58a67a-0bd4-5b27-3d00-9908100e09c1
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: They represent a thread of identity that runs through time and often across distinct representations.

highlight.name: 7768c5d6-c055-775a-13f2-76d7fb182587
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: An object defined primarily by its identity is called an ENTITY.1 ENTITIES have special modeling and design considerations. They have life cycles that can radically change their form and content, but a thread of continuity must be maintained. Their identities must be defined so that they can be effectively tracked. Their class definitions, responsibilities, attributes, and associations should revolve around who they are, rather than the particular attributes they carry. Even for ENTITIES that don’t transform so radically or have such complicated life cycles, placing them in the semantic category leads to more lucid models and more robust implementations.

squiggly.name: 104a3fcb-ffd2-a201-6c0c-7b74e7dcb9d0
squiggly.title: Guest
squiggly.subject: 曲线
squiggly.contents: Deposits and cash withdrawals, which don’t have an identifying number, can be trickier, but the same principle applies: each transaction is an ENTITY, which appears in at least two forms.

highlight.name: 7ab46f80-35f0-5845-1743-7816440e896e
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: When an object is distinguished by its identity, rather than its attributes, make this primary to its definition in the model. Keep the class definition simple and focused on life cycle continuity and identity. Define a means of distinguishing each object regardless of its form or history. Be alert to requirements that call for matching objects by attributes. Define an operation that is guaranteed to produce a unique result for each object, possibly by attaching a symbol that is guaranteed unique. This means of identification may come from the outside, or it may be an arbitrary identifier created by and for the system, but it must correspond to the identity distinctions in the model. The model must define what it means to be the same thing.

highlight.name: 1e3f97c9-84b9-e98c-28de-5c425ed83df2
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: intrinsic

highlight.name: 2e62a169-1318-90c2-16cb-364df5ef18e0
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: superimposed

highlight.name: 9ec52a59-45fa-a051-9958-cf188459eebf
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Rather than focusing on the attributes or even the behavior, strip the ENTITY object’s definition down to the most intrinsic characteristics, particularly those that 78

highlight.name: 4a2c7db7-712e-633b-398d-efd4c2144959
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Add only behavior that is essential to the concept and attributes that are required by that behavior. Beyond that, look to remove behavior and attributes into other objects associated with the core ENTITY. Some of these will be other ENTITIES. Some will be VALUE OBJECTS, which is the next pattern in this chapter. Beyond identity issues, ENTITIES tend to fulfill their responsibilities by coordinating the operations of objects they own.

underline.name: 6ad411fd-7d5c-24c1-6476-85d3cf2f9c40
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: In this example, the phone and address attributes moved into Customer

highlight.name: 18265489-793e-36c7-df09-f95980d918da
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: For example, if a Customer has many contact phone numbers for different purposes, then the phone number is not associated with identity and should stay with the Sales Contact.

highlight.name: 7821e587-18c2-4ec3-3345-7a0ece945416
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: As mentioned earlier, object-oriented languages have “identity” operations that determine if two references point to the same object by comparing the objects’ locations in memory. This kind of identity tracking is too fragile for our purposes.

highlight.name: bea1022d-63d2-adf9-8bb1-77ba6247a165
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Daily newspapers, for example, might be identified by the name of the newspaper, the city, and the date of publication.

highlight.name: b4bb52b8-87fa-9759-cc69-59e7eea789f5
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: designated

highlight.name: 21a2455e-9169-14bd-b1e6-baa94259b7b4
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: For example, the ID attribute is preserved as the object gets flattened into a database and reconstructed. Sometimes a technical framework helps with this process, but otherwise it just takes engineering discipline.

highlight.name: 66df8cad-31a9-a9a4-c949-3bc3ccc02126
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The goal here is to point out when the considerations arise, so that developers are aware they have a problem to solve and know how to narrow down their concerns to the critical areas. The key is to recognize that identity concerns hinge on specific aspects of the model. Often, the means of identification demand a careful study of the domain, as well.

highlight.name: c9579204-c50e-9bed-220c-c62afa2ba01a
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: When I ship a package through a parcel delivery service, I’m given a tracking number, generated by the shipping company’s software, which I can use to identify and follow up on my package. When I book airline tickets or reserve a hotel, I’m given confirmation numbers that are unique identifiers for the transaction.

highlight.name: e1ceaff0-505a-6759-12bb-5d3bfa738665
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Given all these technical problems, it is easy to lose sight of the underlying conceptual problem: What does it mean for two objects to be the same thing? It is easy enough to stamp each object with an ID, or to write an operation that compares two instances, but if these IDs or operations don’t correspond to some meaningful distinction in the domain, they just confuse matters more. This is why identity-assigning operations often involve human input. Checkbook reconciliation software, for instance, may offer likely matches, but the user 80 is expected to make the final determination.

highlight.name: da31ecd7-ad77-6e5e-61ce-1a3d8ce817db
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: conspicuous

highlight.name: eff1b460-cc3a-569b-bc82-792fc0596c25
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: cope

highlight.name: a16a58b3-4ba7-6dda-ad49-1f0e079421a6
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: muddles

highlight.name: d725975a-6454-0983-caaa-1fe75138736d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The system has to cope with all that tracking, and many possible performance optimizations are ruled out. Analytical effort is required to define meaningful identities and work out foolproof ways to track objects across distributed systems or in database storage. Equally important, taking on artificial identities is misleading. It muddles the model, forcing all objects into the same mold.

highlight.name: 4dd2a7ce-5b8a-89ce-e417-97b09c6b92dc
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: An object that represents a descriptive aspect of the domain with no conceptual identity is called a VALUE OBJECT. VALUE OBJECTS are instantiated to represent elements of the design that we care about only for what they are, not who or which they are.

highlight.name: 9d555bd2-c54d-97bb-2e50-59e624e52276
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: In software for an electric utility company, an address corresponds to a destination for the company’s lines and service. If roommates each called to order electrical service, the company would need to realize it. Address is an ENTITY. Alternatively, the model could associate utility service with a “dwelling,” an ENTITY with an attribute of address. Then Address would be a VALUE OBJECT.

highlight.name: c52341a0-e1fc-f195-e0ab-61e6c7afcce6
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A VALUE OBJECT can be an assemblage of other objects. In software for designing house plans, an object could be created for each window style. This “window style” could be incorporated into a “window” object, along with height and width, as well as rules governing how these attributes can be changed and combined. These windows are intricate VALUE OBJECTS made up of other VALUE OBJECTS. They in turn would be incorporated into larger elements of a plan, such as “wall” objects.

highlight.name: 208431c1-8634-e358-e54d-d3984178d7f1
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: VALUE OBJECTS are often passed as parameters in messages between objects. They are frequently transient, created for an operation and then discarded. VALUE OBJECTS are used as attributes of ENTITIES (and other VALUES). A person may be modeled as an ENTITY with an identity, but that person’s name is a VALUE.

highlight.name: e749fdae-095a-7300-ae6e-ca47e8e8e754
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The attributes that make up a VALUE OBJECT should form a conceptual whole.2 For example, street, city, and postal code shouldn’t be separate attributes of a Person object. They are part of a single, whole address, which makes a simpler Person, and a more coherent VALUE OBJECT.

highlight.name: c11eb5d9-03cc-ca26-4c83-d66575374484
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The economy of copying versus sharing depends on the implementation environment. Although copies may clog the system with huge numbers of objects, sharing can slow down a distributed system. When a copy is passed between two machines, a single message is sent and the copy lives independently on the receiving machine. But if a single instance is being shared, only a reference is passed, requiring a message back to the 83 object for each interaction.

highlight.name: 83f47661-050e-88ea-53c0-be52567d343b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It just means that more discipline is needed to maintain the rules that will be only implicit in the implementation. This can be reinforced with naming conventions, selective documentation, and lots of discussion.

highlight.name: a82697f0-bb3d-1c6c-7407-442b8355a083
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Defining VALUE OBJECTS and designating them as immutable is a case of following a general rule: Avoiding unnecessary constraints in a model leaves developers free to do purely technical performance tuning. Explicitly defining the essential constraints lets developers tweak the design while keeping safe from changing meaningful behavior. Such design tweaks are often very specific to the technology in use on a particular project.

highlight.name: abe3b8a3-677b-d970-d60d-7d055d06eb79
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The most you could say is that it points to an object that is equal to the one pointing to it, but you would have to enforce that invariant somewhere.

highlight.name: 62de58ee-db3f-bcf2-0359-7a0bed23a5d1
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: In some cases, the clearest and most pragmatic design includes operations that do not conceptually belong to any object. Rather than force the issue, we can follow the natural contours of the problem space and include 85 SERVICES explicitly in the model.

highlight.name: 2e560642-e82f-a596-9cee-01471d3a70b9
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Some concepts from the domain aren’t natural to model as objects. Forcing the required domain functionality to be the responsibility of an ENTITY or VALUE either distorts the definition of a model-based object or adds meaningless artificial objects.

highlight.name: 2773b899-379a-a404-7945-8b55c9eace8b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A SERVICE can still have an abstract, intentional definition; it just has a different flavor than the definition of an object.

highlight.name: f033affe-feee-b250-6029-b921976caaa6
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: SERVICES should be used judiciously and not allowed to strip the ENTITIES and VALUE OBJECTS of all their behavior. But when an operation is actually an important domain concept, a SERVICE forms a natural part of a MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN. Declared in the model as a SERVICE, rather than as a phony object that doesn’t actually represent anything, the standalone operation will not mislead anyone.

highlight.name: ad9acbe0-a61e-d998-2e0b-65ce859249a2
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: 1. The operation relates to a domain concept that is not a natural part of an ENTITY or VALUE OBJECT. 2. The interface is defined in terms of other elements of the domain model. 3. The operation is stateless.

highlight.name: 32c85152-d12e-4e69-5a23-8058368b2b40
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Statelessness here means that any client can use any instance of a particular SERVICE without regard to the instance’s individual history. The execution of a SERVICE will use information that is accessible globally, and may even change that global information (that is, it may have side effects). But the SERVICE does not hold state of its own that affects its own behavior, as most domain objects do.

highlight.name: 1038f4f0-d5d9-a5f1-000e-f48672257e09
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Most SERVICES discussed in the literature are purely technical and belong in the infrastructure layer. Domain and application SERVICES collaborate with these infrastructure SERVICES. For example, a bank might have an application that sends an e-mail to a customer when an account balance falls below a specific threshold. The interface that encapsulates the e-mail system, and perhaps alternate means of notification, is a SERVICE in the infrastructure layer.

highlight.name: 2a40966d-10ad-1f4d-581f-7989fba31dbc
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It can be harder to distinguish application SERVICES from domain SERVICES. The application layer is responsible for ordering the notification. The domain layer is responsible for determining if a threshold was met—though this task probably does not call for a SERVICE, because it would fit the responsibility of an “account” object. That banking application could be responsible for funds transfers. If a SERVICE were devised to make appropriate debits and credits for a funds transfer, that capability would belong in the domain layer. Funds transfer has a meaning in the banking domain language, and it involves fundamental business logic. Technical SERVICES should lack any business meaning at all.

highlight.name: f5d594a6-90f5-d4b0-97e4-38add65cc540
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: We might like to create a Funds Transfer object to represent the two entries plus the rules and history around the transfer. But we are still left with calls to SERVICES in the interbank networks. What’s more, in most development systems, it is awkward to make a direct interface between a domain object and external resources. We can dress up such external SERVICES with a FACADE that takes inputs in terms of the model, perhaps returning a Funds Transfer object as its result. But whatever intermediaries we might have, and even though they don’t belong to us, those SERVICES are carrying out the domain responsibility of funds transfer.

highlight.name: fd1ef860-7b0a-7e34-69a2-1d884787c46f
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: As previously discussed, fine-grained domain objects can contribute to knowledge leaks from the domain into the application layer, where the domain object’s behavior is coordinated. The complexity of a highly detailed interaction ends up being handled in the application layer, allowing domain knowledge to creep into the application or user interface code, where it is lost from the domain layer. The judicious introduction of domain services can help maintain the bright line between layers.

highlight.name: b4dadc83-7321-21e3-d79c-74207b54d1d8
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Distributed system architectures, such as J2EE and CORBA, provide special publishing mechanisms for SERVICES, with conventions for their use, and they add distribution and access capabilities. But such frameworks are not always in use on a project, and even when they are, they are likely to be overkill when the motivation is just a logical separation of concerns.

highlight.name: 367ff284-2fea-a3ff-39cb-1fe29a24845c
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The means of providing access to a SERVICE is not as important as the design decision to carve off specific responsibilities. A “doer” object may be satisfactory as an implementation of a SERVICE’s interface. A simple SINGLETON (Gamma et al. 1995) can be written easily to provide access. Coding conventions can make it clear that these objects are just delivery mechanisms for SERVICE interfaces, and not meaningful domain objects. Elaborate architectures should be used only when there is a real need to distribute the system or otherwise draw on the framework’s capabilities.

highlight.name: 164970b5-5508-1991-477b-f2c3305bd46c
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: MODULES are an old, established design element. There are technical considerations, but cognitive overload is 88 the primary motivation for modularity. MODULES give people two views of the model: They can look at detail within a MODULE without being overwhelmed by the whole, or they can look at relationships between MODULES in views that exclude interior detail.

highlight.name: efe1eb12-d469-dcbd-2986-9ded56d79442
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Everyone uses MODULES, but few treat them as a full-fledged part of the model. Code gets broken down into all sorts of categories, from aspects of the technical architecture to developers’ work assignments. Even developers who refactor a lot tend to content themselves with MODULES conceived early in the project.

highlight.name: d4393fdb-1b38-0df2-f0ee-815dae131cbf
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: MODULES and the smaller elements should coevolve, but typically they do not. MODULES are chosen to organize an early form of the objects. After that, the objects tend to change in ways that keep them in the bounds of the existing MODULE definition. Refactoring MODULES is more work and more disruptive than refactoring classes, and probably can’t be as frequent. But just as model objects tend to start out naive and concrete and then gradually transform to reveal deeper insight, MODULES can become subtle and abstract. Letting the MODULES reflect changing understanding of the domain will also allow more freedom for the objects within them to evolve.

highlight.name: 050dfab5-ec7f-a0ea-985d-317637520852
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Looking at conceptual relationships is not an alternative to technical measures. They are different levels of the same issue, and both have to be accomplished. But model-focused thinking produces a deeper solution, rather than an incidental one. And when there has to be a trade-off, it is best to go with the conceptual clarity, even if it means more references between MODULES or occasional ripple effects when changes are made to a MODULE. Developers can handle these problems if they understand the story the model is telling them.

highlight.name: 1bae0e82-f4a1-dfb5-103c-c986063b83e1
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: If an individual class really does depend on a specific class in another package, and the local MODULE doesn’t seem to have a conceptual dependency on the other MODULE, then maybe a class should be moved, or the MODULES themselves should be reconsidered.

highlight.name: b4b02db4-3c17-73be-9bee-a33546b2c2bb
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: On the other hand, tiered architectures can fragment the implementation of the model objects. Some frameworks create tiers by spreading the responsibilities of a single domain object across multiple objects and then placing those objects in separate packages. For example, with J2EE a common practice is to place data and data access into an “entity bean” while placing associated business logic into a “session bean.” In addition to the increased implementation complexity of each component, the separation immediately robs an object model of cohesion. One of the most fundamental concepts of objects is to encapsulate data with the logic that operates on that data. This kind of tiered implementation is not fatal, because both components can be viewed as together constituting the implementation of a single model element, but to make matters worse, the entity and session beans are often separated into different packages. At that point, viewing the various objects and mentally fitting them back together as a single conceptual ENTITY is just too much effort. We lose the connection between the model and design. Best practice is to use EJBs at a larger grain than ENTITY objects, reducing the downside of separating tiers. But fine-grain objects are often split into tiers also.

highlight.name: c6e068cb-c5b5-4fc1-de7c-34bb88586012
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: This kind of framework design is attempting to address two legitimate issues. One is the logical division of concerns: One object has responsibility for database access, another for business logic, and so on. Such divisions make it easier to understand the functioning of each tier (on a technical level) and make it easier to switch out layers. The trouble is that the cost to application development is not recognized. This is not a book on framework design, so I won’t go into alternative solutions to that problem, but they do exist. And even if there were no options, it would be better to trade off these benefits for a more cohesive domain layer.

highlight.name: 52c30440-147c-3a16-8750-91d2feb5055f
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The other motivation for these packaging schemes is the distribution of tiers. This could be a strong argument if the code actually got deployed on different servers. Usually it does not. The flexibility is sought just in case it is needed. On a project that hopes to get leverage from MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN, this sacrifice is too great unless it solves an immediate and pressing problem.

highlight.name: 766c39b0-83a4-a999-5b83-b04a605cef88
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: There are other pitfalls where framework design or just conventions of a company or project can undermine MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN by obscuring the natural cohesion of the domain objects, but the bottom line is the same. The restrictions, or just the large number of required packages, rules out the use of other packaging schemes that are tailored to the needs of the domain model.

highlight.name: e162142a-fa86-7cce-2e70-c722f3c02bab
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: anemic domain model

underline.name: 45c596fb-d41b-222a-47cc-11235d4d9f6d
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN calls for an implementation technology in tune with the particular modeling paradigm being applied.

underline.name: 80c5b52f-9d00-a9ee-b910-d5ba27a8038b
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: This predominance has come about for a variety of reasons: some factors are intrinsic to objects, some are circumstantial, and others derive from the advantages that come from wide usage itself.

underline.name: 8652f902-583c-a829-abbf-2015d86158b8
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: object modeling does strike a nice balance of simplicity and sophistication.

underline.name: d8708acb-f297-caf6-0054-91b0ec0efc56
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: If the nontechnical members of the team can’t grasp at least the rudiments of the paradigm, they will not understand the model, and the UBIQUITOUS LANGUAGE will be lost.

underline.name: 542f0591-0bd2-6e3c-06f5-82d981d2b690
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: Without mature infrastructure and tool support, a project can get sidetracked into technological R&D, delaying and diverting resources away from application development and introducing technical risks.

underline.name: 854c412d-c2bc-6a9a-2e4c-6dcf97e83660
underline.title: Guest
underline.subject: 下划线
underline.contents: But over the years, many of these problems have been solved for objects, or made irrelevant by widespread adoption.

highlight.name: af31b983-a4ed-f52f-eb92-f9629c97dcba
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: feasible

highlight.name: a323c469-dbe7-01c2-065a-503c3acdc7c0
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: gelled

highlight.name: ec37aeb1-02d3-3341-f4c1-f0621a2a74d4
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It may not be feasible to educate developers in a 93 reasonable amount of time because the patterns for making the most of the paradigm and technology haven’t gelled yet. Perhaps the pioneers of the field are effective but haven’t yet published their insights in an accessible form.

highlight.name: 31d047b9-d465-31b8-0c58-07f8da2d0a4c
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Fortunately, we were able to bring onto the team one of a handful of people in the world with the skills to extricate us from the problem.

highlight.name: 6d96e9b5-3bc0-8b2d-d279-fd28af067945
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Third, parts of the object model had such a tangle of interdependencies that contention became a problem with a relatively small number of concurrent transactions.

highlight.name: c253aab1-0e04-3162-4606-24cbc1ee7946
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Sadly, this project eventually retrenched and became quite conservative.

highlight.name: c0ea5cf4-079e-35ba-7c7b-3c90c1bec673
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: To this day they use the exotic technologies, but for cautiously scoped applications that probably don’t really benefit from them.

highlight.name: 0a928a67-6dfa-8460-0eab-c29ac812a1a8
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A decade later, object-oriented technology is relatively mature. Most common infrastructure needs can be met with off-the-shelf solutions that have been used in the field.

highlight.name: 8a5ba8ae-2868-9c01-552f-f86f2d0f94ed
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Mission-critical tools come from major vendors, often multiple vendors, or from stable open-source projects.

highlight.name: 7e81c5b1-fb05-af72-da62-d67929b43db6
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Traveling with the crowd provides some safety, but it isn’t always the way to go. Object models address a large number of practical software problems, but there are domains that are not natural to model as discrete packets of encapsulated behavior.

highlight.name: 5ef5a7bc-fc94-d6e0-badb-a77b348ca0ad
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: specialties.

highlight.name: b859a5a1-7f5f-a1fc-8cb2-e3462bb1571b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: When there are just a few anomalous elements of a domain that otherwise works well in a paradigm, developers can live with a few awkward objects in an otherwise consistent model.

highlight.name: f1577172-3773-0179-29e0-6c1193ea5529
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Sticking with MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN When Mixing Paradigms

highlight.name: fdbeb87a-6a75-8c12-02bd-08058b070a25
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Some lack a seamless view that can show the relatedness of model concepts that run between the two implementation environments. One common outcome is an application fractured in two: a static data storage system using objects, and an ad hoc rules processing application that has lost almost all connection with the object model.

highlight.name: fe3258ee-b3f9-2ab4-1c79-4ba4aa846c4e
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Lean on the ubiquitous language. Even when there is no rigorous connection between tools, very consistent use of language can keep parts of the design from diverging.

highlight.name: f3116dcd-d78f-fe93-af20-636980c17dc0
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: intimately

highlight.name: 09fda1e3-cf45-8e8f-bf0f-14005ca3f7ac
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Although a MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN does not have to be object oriented, it does depend on having an expressive implementation of the model constructs, be they objects, rules, or workflows. If the available tool does not facilitate that expressiveness, reconsider the choice of tools. An unexpressive implementation negates the advantage of the extra paradigm.

highlight.name: e6c27140-cd2b-fce1-cbc8-5940b2f7c586
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: thumb

highlight.name: 5fac0097-44e1-b26f-9760-06b14478c2cc
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Before taking on the burden of mixed paradigms, the options within the dominant paradigm should be exhausted.

highlight.name: c0fc2f46-cfe6-3e9d-d10a-40574fe297ea
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Of course, many of these are simple, transient objects, created with an easy call to their constructor, used in some computation, and then abandoned to the garbage collector. There is no need to complicate such objects. But other objects have longer lives, not all of which are spent in active memory. They have complex interdependencies with other objects. They go through changes of state to which invariants apply. Managing these objects presents challenges that can easily derail an attempt at MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN.

highlight.name: 79e58bd4-01f3-2e8d-21ad-4c139e5ed8fd
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Next, the focus turns to the beginning of the life cycle, using FACTORIES to create and reconstitute complex objects and AGGREGATES, keeping their internal structure encapsulated. Finally, REPOSITORIES address the middle and end of the life cycle, providing the means of finding and retrieving persistent objects while encapsulating the immense infrastructure involved.

highlight.name: 6abeb2b2-d31d-68e1-1d54-4499806e8244
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: AGGREGATES mark off the scope within which invariants have to be maintained at every stage of the life cycle.

highlight.name: 090c5c42-7916-f308-f614-55d10951d82c
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It is difficult to guarantee the consistency of changes to objects in a model with complex associations. Invariants need to be maintained that apply to closely related groups of objects, not just discrete objects. Yet cautious locking schemes cause multiple users to interfere pointlessly with each other and make a system unusable.

highlight.name: 7732a263-f694-9f8e-33b9-e87ba8b85cbf
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: hacking and hoping

highlight.name: 68545e2b-ca2b-eaad-0c95-28a259a8b9a9
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: We need to find a model that leaves high-contention points looser and strict invariants tighter.

highlight.name: 6f78104d-b601-6ab8-af0b-83d18636cb56
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: rotation

highlight.name: a3a8e841-1c84-ee96-d536-b48741f69a15
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Although this problem surfaces as technical difficulties in database transactions, it is rooted in the model—in its lack of defined boundaries. A solution driven from the model will make the model easier to understand and make the design easier to communicate. As the model is revised, it will guide our changes to the implementation.

highlight.name: b2820e23-5281-6c8e-c2f6-c8289cc6c6f5
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Each AGGREGATE has a root and a boundary.

highlight.name: 3a6240ce-8176-a356-4b38-a3859d0d5e17
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The root is the only member of the AGGREGATE that outside objects are allowed to hold references to, although objects within the boundary may hold references to each other. ENTITIES other than the root have local identity, but that identity needs to be distinguishable only within the AGGREGATE, because no outside object can ever see it out of the context of the root ENTITY.

highlight.name: cc6f927b-9f54-4226-0b97-20104a8b0492
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Invariants, which are consistency rules that must be maintained whenever data changes, will involve relationships between members of the AGGREGATE. Any rule that spans AGGREGATES will not be expected to be up-to-date at all times. Through event processing, batch processing, or other update mechanisms, other dependencies can be resolved within some specified time. But the invariants applied within an AGGREGATE will be enforced with the completion of each transaction.

highlight.name: 71333fb7-26da-0b7e-5179-973bab65d7cf
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Nothing outside the AGGREGATE boundary can hold a reference to anything inside, except to the root ENTITY. The root ENTITY can hand references to the internal ENTITIES to other objects, but those objects can use them only transiently, and they may not hold on to the reference. The root may hand a copy of a VALUE OBJECT to another object, and it doesn’t matter what happens to it, because it’s just a VALUE and no longer will have any association with the AGGREGATE.

highlight.name: 5d72d7bd-0a50-a7d4-dee4-eb0e7ad227ca
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: corollary

highlight.name: 106da4c0-6a2e-b449-de84-821a7bfb8ca3
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Transient references to internal members can be passed out for use within a single operation only. Because the root controls access, it cannot be blindsided by changes to the internals.

highlight.name: 7308e565-2a87-f4e1-290d-56841af11081
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Clearly, locking a single line item isn’t an adequate safeguard. If instead we had locked an entire PO at a time, the problem would have been prevented.

highlight.name: f3b96adb-7799-b59c-6475-6d8d6ab01361
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Even assuming many small POs, there are other ways to violate the assertion. Consider that “part.” If someone changed the price of a trombone while Amanda was adding to her order, wouldn’t that violate the invariant too?

highlight.name: d348e239-1196-d852-22ff-97285aa7cf2e
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: neither locked line item is involved in the other user’s change.

highlight.name: 5f568845-cf6a-1356-521e-53a55471ab1d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Let’s try locking the part in addition to the entire PO.

highlight.name: 3012903b-b355-3caf-a5d6-334e40eed49c
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: They should, of course, show the prices as of the time they were filled, rather than current prices.

highlight.name: 0e353621-81e4-2976-ce62-8304c925c2ea
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The AGGREGATE imposes an ownership of the PO and its items that is consistent with business practice. The creation and deletion of a PO and items are naturally tied together, while the creation and deletion of parts is independent.

highlight.name: 6935b9de-86b2-1340-fb41-7af01bda04e2
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: At the same time, tightening the relationship of the PO and its line items guarantees that an important business rule will be followed.

highlight.name: c83657d1-37af-c4e1-e421-d70664731f8b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Much of the power of objects rests in the intricate configuration of their internals and their associations. An object should be distilled until nothing remains that does not relate to its meaning or support its role in interactions. This mid-life cycle responsibility is plenty. Problems arise from overloading a complex object with responsibility for its own creation.

highlight.name: 8210e692-10d5-12b4-e25a-735f8aa76e23
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: One could imagine trying to design an engine block that could grab on to a set of pistons and insert them into its cylinders, spark plugs that would find their sockets and screw themselves in.

highlight.name: c9ed9494-3b16-7a33-ef4d-cb0b11c3f1b1
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: But shifting responsibility to the other interested party, the client object in the application, leads to even worse problems.

highlight.name: bbc8f986-af66-14e0-e54d-1f6f3c230088
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Even calling constructors couples the client to the concrete classes of the objects it is building. No change to the implementation of the domain objects can be made without changing the client, making refactoring harder.

highlight.name: 711a5feb-8de4-810a-47e7-59c6804237aa
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A client taking on object creation becomes unnecessarily complicated and blurs its responsibility.

highlight.name: 54174937-c834-e7b5-d91a-008b7cfdd2cd
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A program element whose responsibility is the creation of other objects is called a FACTORY.

highlight.name: a4a8b0f0-5753-a060-c6e6-1f3035f14a98
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Each creation method is atomic and enforces all invariants of the created object or AGGREGATE.

highlight.name: ccfe3446-4584-295f-17fd-4287875f9fdf
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: For an ENTITY, this means the creation of the entire AGGREGATE, with all invariants satisfied, but probably with optional elements still to be added.

highlight.name: e4ccc9b2-8b42-f57b-780e-19208f528195
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: For an immutable VALUE OBJECT, this means that all attributes are initialized to their correct final state.

highlight.name: 912ec389-e12d-8a87-93a1-5419c9664146
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The FACTORY should be abstracted to the type desired, rather than the concrete class(es) created. The sophisticated FACTORY patterns in Gamma et al. 1995 help with this.

highlight.name: 9fef04e2-169d-3d7b-364c-5927c43613d0
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: For example, if you needed to add elements inside a preexisting AGGREGATE, you might create a FACTORY METHOD on the root of the AGGREGATE. This hides the implementation of the interior of the AGGREGATE from any external client, while giving the root responsibility for ensuring the integrity of the AGGREGATE as elements are added, as shown in Figure 6.13 on the next page.

highlight.name: b7d5b857-c5e5-e112-9e7d-fbe16e38e779
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Another example would be to place a FACTORY METHOD on an object that is closely involved in spawning another object, although it doesn’t own the product once it is created. When the data and possibly the rules of one object are very dominant in the creation of an object, this saves pulling information out of the spawner to be used elsewhere to create the object. It also communicates the special relationship between the spawner and the product.

highlight.name: 5b36f5a1-de32-dc6e-b411-22b5209f31ed
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: In Figure 6.14, the Trade Order is not part of the same AGGREGATE as the Brokerage Account because, for a start, it will go on to interact with the trade execution application, where the Brokerage Account would only be in the way. Even so, it seems natural to give the Brokerage Account control over the creation of Trade Orders. The Brokerage Account contains information that will be embedded in the Trade Order (starting with its own identity), and it contains rules that govern what trades are allowed. We might also benefit from hiding the implementation of Trade Order. For example, it might be refactored into a hierarchy, with separate subclasses for Buy Order and Sell Order. The FACTORY keeps the client from being coupled to the concrete classes.

highlight.name: d08733f7-e3a5-d4e8-0fb9-fb63b33db7dd
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The Java class library offers interesting examples. All collections implement interfaces that decouple the client from the concrete implementation. Yet they are all created by direct calls to constructors. A FACTORY could have encapsulated the collection hierarchy. The FACTORY’s methods could have allowed a client to ask for the features it needed, with the FACTORY selecting the appropriate class to instantiate. Code that created collections would be more expressive, and new collection classes could be installed without breaking every Java program.

highlight.name: 074e265b-cd1b-4bfa-efda-a52fbaac9374
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The abstract collection types preserve some value in spite of the lack of a FACTORY because of their usage patterns. Collections are very often created in one place and used in another. This means that the client that ultimately uses the collection—adding, removing, and retrieving its contents—can still talk to the interface and be decoupled from the implementation. The selection of a collection class typically falls to the object that owns the collection, or to the owning object’s FACTORY.

highlight.name: a49a767f-a375-6d50-ef25-3707b4816f30
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The safest parameters are those from a lower design layer. Even within a layer, there tend to be natural strata with more basic objects that are used by higher level objects.

highlight.name: d946c699-1c97-ab69-4b0e-4055449ee0f0
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The FACTORY is coupled to the concrete class of the products; it does not need to be coupled to concrete parameters also.

highlight.name: 75de95e8-56ba-5f0f-478d-51a00399d1d5
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Under some circumstances, there are advantages to placing invariant logic in the FACTORY and reducing clutter in the product. This is especially appealing with AGGREGATE rules (which span many objects). It is especially unappealing with FACTORY METHODS attached to other domain objects.

highlight.name: 0d7376a1-cb0d-4c75-aff2-3df6ebbe20ed
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Although in principle invariants apply at the end of every operation, often the transformations allowed to the object can never bring them into play. There might be a rule that applies to the assignment of the identity attributes of an ENTITY. But after creation that identity is immutable. VALUE OBJECTS are completely immutable. An object doesn’t need to carry around logic that will never be applied in its active lifetime. In such cases, the FACTORY is a logical place to put invariants, keeping the product simpler.

highlight.name: 817e557c-095e-5571-3fe7-8967cd987066
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Most transmission methods flatten an object into an 111 even more limited presentation. Therefore, retrieval requires a potentially complex process of reassembling the parts into a live object.

highlight.name: 6a47ffa4-abe6-2fa4-ece6-44605b8d42f3
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A FACTORY reconstituting an object will handle violation of an invariant differently. During creation of a new object, a FACTORY should simply balk when an invariant isn’t met, but a more flexible response may be necessary in reconstitution. If an object already exists somewhere in the system (such as in the database), this fact cannot be ignored. Yet we also can’t ignore the rule violation. There has to be some strategy for repairing such inconsistencies, which can make reconstitution more challenging than the creation of new objects.

highlight.name: 92d75446-812f-f624-1441-90c1e4afc3b4
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Associations allow us to find an object based on its relationship to another. But we must have a starting point for a traversal to an ENTITY or VALUE in the middle of its life cycle.

highlight.name: 2e7f284f-4688-35b7-6bac-257bc2d7f459
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: This self-imposed limitation forced them to create just the kind of endless tangle that we have been trying to avert over the last few chapters, with careful implementation of ENTITIES and application of AGGREGATES.

highlight.name: a5d403ac-44df-757a-7bb9-6578654cf9c7
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Few would even think of this approach, much less be tempted by it

highlight.name: 1ac36a1f-65f6-1b18-9213-6cf58fda4b7b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: constituents

highlight.name: 1a1890e6-240d-8b4e-1e61-254eb45675f3
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Whether to provide a traversal or depend on a search becomes a design decision, trading off the decoupling of the search against the cohesiveness of the association.

highlight.name: 6a5913dd-425c-d6d9-6f2a-369ff1305c5d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: To keep this distinction in mind, I refer to the creation of an instance from stored data as reconstitution.

highlight.name: 6196b284-0ce3-1a8a-88ef-613cf1bfda81
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It becomes natural to think of the objects as containers for the data that the queries provide, and the whole design shifts toward a data-processing style.

highlight.name: 5eec9622-92d0-8b12-e48f-c35197170f3b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The details of the technology vary

highlight.name: 0d7d52d0-36fa-713e-3b4a-f7d17d6a75bc
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A client needs a practical means of acquiring references to preexisting domain objects.

highlight.name: 4040b5a6-0cd8-e553-6af9-c7a4b6b7aac1
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: We also need no query access for persistent objects that are more convenient to find by traversal. For example, the address of a person could be requested from the Person object. And most important, any object internal to an AGGREGATE is prohibited from access except by traversal from the root.

highlight.name: df3dd50c-e42d-1e0e-57f1-4e7ef98ba933
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: itineraries

highlight.name: 1e060e27-e0cd-d007-9dce-a2012f52ffbf
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: For example, when I am planning travel online, I sometimes save a few prospective itineraries and return later to select one to book. Those itineraries are VALUES (if there were two made up of the same flights, I would not care which was which), but they have been associated with my user name and retrieved for me intact. Another case would be an “enumeration,” when a type has a strictly limited, predetermined set of possible values. Global access to VALUE OBJECTS is much less common than for ENTITIES, though, and if you find you need to search the database for a preexisting VALUE, it is worth considering the possibility that you’ve really got an ENTITY whose identity you haven’t recognized.

highlight.name: 4a9d302a-2195-3365-86f5-cb648d4e3efd
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A subset of persistent objects must be globally accessible through a search based on object attributes. Such access is needed for the roots of AGGREGATES that are not convenient to reach by traversal. They are usually ENTITIES, sometimes VALUE OBJECTS with complex internal structure, and sometimes enumerated VALUES. Providing access to other objects muddies important distinctions. Free database queries can actually breach the encapsulation of domain objects and AGGREGATES. Exposure of technical infrastructure and database access mechanisms complicates the client and obscures the MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN.

highlight.name: 2bffa3aa-9103-a308-7ff4-e4f080eee70b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: raft

highlight.name: a7ef97d6-93a1-81b9-ca1a-c7e755d99165
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: elaborate

highlight.name: c8077d82-3f12-f5b1-b64c-51d3e2350a2b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: machinery

highlight.name: 6d6e9bc6-2a86-e9e2-729e-4cbc37e825bb
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A REPOSITORY lifts a huge burden from the client, which can now talk to a simple, intention-revealing interface, and ask for what it needs in terms of the model.

highlight.name: 15a3bf25-7ba0-b402-d172-fdd0e182ff6b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: For each type of object that needs global access, create an object that can provide the illusion of an in-memory collection of all objects of that type.

highlight.name: 3823ed90-e2a3-b479-6b57-0f4610a7e114
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: They allow easy substitution of a dummy implementation, for use in testing (typically using an in- memory collection).

highlight.name: c689ae6b-742f-ae01-e073-102f98a6b0b3
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: These queries can be various: retrieving an ENTITY by its identity (provided by almost all REPOSITORIES); requesting a collection of objects with a particular attribute value or a complex combination of parameters; selecting objects based on value ranges (such as date ranges); and even performing some calculations that fall within the general responsibility of a REPOSITORY

highlight.name: 42c2be40-6435-f962-102d-572740689b3f
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: (especially drawing on operations supported by the underlying database)

highlight.name: 91e39910-b919-c8db-26ac-f59127fdbd23
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: One particularly apt approach to generalizing REPOSITORIES through a framework is to use SPECIFICATION-based queries. A SPECIFICATION allows a client to describe (that is, specify) what it wants without concern for how it will be obtained. In the process, an object that can actually carry out the selection is created. This pattern will be discussed in depth in Chapter 9.

highlight.name: bf5eb359-deff-6afd-1158-15fb80846dfd
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Hard-coded queries can be built on top of any infrastructure and without a lot of investment, because they do just what some client would have had to do anyway.

highlight.name: 51978987-049a-1e52-ce07-97d81ecaaffc
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: This calls for a staff familiar with the necessary technology and is greatly aided by a supportive infrastructure.

highlight.name: bc3ab42a-41ea-d03d-08dc-42b846c849e0
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: prohibitively

highlight.name: 7e1f2a95-d1d9-2e6e-0c8f-db80c6c423f2
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Frameworks that don’t allow for such contingencies tend to distort the domain design or get bypassed by developers.

highlight.name: d9a49bc8-d476-4f69-40eb-c5506f205050
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The performance implications can be extreme when REPOSITORIES are used in different ways or work in different ways.

highlight.name: 0b80d08c-0d77-f704-b4ad-df59ef7044a7
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The system was mysteriously running out of memory after a few hours of use

highlight.name: 9f603050-1cf0-b235-6c19-82067dcd26a6
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: underlying

highlight.name: 20693704-1aed-4608-2763-d058bb23321d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Keep in mind that you may well face constraints imposed by the lack of such polymorphism in your database technology.

highlight.name: 2d16c109-d0d0-e2e9-4d1c-52dc64601064
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Although the REPOSITORY will insert into and delete from the database, it will ordinarily not commit anything. It is tempting to commit after saving, for example, but the client presumably has the context to correctly initiate and commit units of work. Transaction management will be simpler if the REPOSITORY keeps its hands off.

highlight.name: b4c5a633-b8a4-63bf-510f-ea9edfc7715a
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: But of course, this will have to be done with queries that return collections anyway in Java.

highlight.name: 7f8e8b2a-16e1-75a9-5e46-abc38433a523
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: For example, your project might be committed to J2EE.

highlight.name: 42617c2d-c06c-1307-c88d-ed5d2eb43ddc
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Looking for conceptual affinities between the framework and the patterns of MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN

highlight.name: aec88017-df46-a729-7aa1-658462a02f4b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: antagonistic.

highlight.name: bcd76ed4-7eb4-390d-c7fc-e1a80965880c
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The FACTORY makes new objects; the REPOSITORY finds old objects.

highlight.name: 9cce2564-ce81-30a5-54dc-1dfb4695a91b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: These two views can be reconciled by making the REPOSITORY delegate object creation to a FACTORY, which (in theory, though seldom in practice) could also be used to create objects from scratch.

highlight.name: 2826bf03-96d5-725f-9ada-a79adcdfb628
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: This clear separation also helps by unloading all responsibility for persistence from the FACTORIES. A FACTORY’S job is to instantiate a potentially complex object from data. If the product is a new object, the client will know this and can add it to the REPOSITORY, which will encapsulate the storage of the object in the database.

highlight.name: 7a032762-2d40-8003-d72d-c1077a90cfce
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It is a minor convenience at best.

highlight.name: bd00172b-a33e-cfa3-4108-c5b17109657d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Usually, the distinction between a new object and an existing object is important in the domain, and a framework that transparently combines them will actually muddle the situation.

highlight.name: 481eabb7-c2f7-489c-ab3f-68b2dfc2c5b5
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The most common nonobject component of primarily object-oriented software systems is the relational database.

highlight.name: 703b2c39-4718-a6f4-ed95-00066d7d5498
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: But the database is more intimately related to the object model than are most other components.

highlight.name: 2e6cb82d-7894-ab5c-53ce-6f3296f30f39
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: There are reasonably refined tools for creating and managing mappings between the two.

highlight.name: 2f1ec646-8eae-0039-cf5b-b20b8913a3d7
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The trouble is, multiple overlapping models are just too complicated.

highlight.name: 000cf3c2-b138-c570-3ba5-873830f19437
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Many of the same arguments presented for MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN—avoiding separate analysis and design models—apply to this mismatch.

highlight.name: 209c1e39-92e8-424e-a5b1-7d4c7061e7c6
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: such as selective denormalization

highlight.name: 6c3ed5be-221a-3e24-8831-bf95c7f38b09
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: But it is crucial that the mappings be transparent, easily understandable by inspecting the code or reading entries in the mapping tool.

highlight.name: bb85bace-a2b0-b7f1-6747-826211bffa44
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: When the database is being viewed as an object store, don’t let the data model and the object model diverge far, regardless of the powers of the mapping tools.

highlight.name: ee34de53-a254-9939-9c8f-c4b72e15332f
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Processes outside the object system should not access such an object store.

highlight.name: 5576cd69-f4bd-a997-b1a4-3a2a55661d8b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It may make sense to conform to the model implicit in the other system, or it may be better to make the model completely distinct.

highlight.name: e18ebaac-e4b1-8991-52a7-2f53531dc3e7
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Another reason for exceptions is performance. Quirky design changes may have to be introduced to solve execution speed problems

highlight.name: bb8cbd7f-da59-68a1-45b1-a1da5a2e21ed
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: A table row should contain an object, perhaps along with subsidiaries in an AGGREGATE.

highlight.name: a81ac828-e355-f950-7ec0-e2fa611e900d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The necessity of sometimes deviating from this simple directness should not lead to total abandonment of the principle of simple mappings.

highlight.name: 9ab25ab5-d016-51bb-4901-1de764d728c9
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The names and associations of elements in the objects should correspond meticulously to those of the relational tables.

highlight.name: f9efab14-bf1e-e382-7053-63241ebd1457
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Cutting the two loose from each other is a seductive path.

highlight.name: e8ca113a-5a3f-6f28-7426-5aa7cb334260
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: If the separation is chosen consciously, it can result in a clean database schema—not an awkward one full of compromises conforming to last year’s object model.

highlight.name: 2cdfed29-6a5c-6f12-fad3-829808694c2d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: elaborate

highlight.name: c7e9e4a8-46e7-e4f8-a541-a416287bf727
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: This class would probably be elaborated into a hierarchy of different kinds of incidents, such as loading, unloading, or being claimed by the receiver.

highlight.name: 43dd373b-d45e-8db8-cd05-dbfb180b17c4
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Cargoes can ride from place to place by being loaded onto Carriers for the duration of one or more Carrier Movements

highlight.name: 1bbf076d-987d-ecc3-ee29-e7ae7dedf06e
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: In order to frame up a solid implementation, this model still needs some clarification and tightening.

highlight.name: 95133f40-e9a1-5055-eedb-312105f4a4c3
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: employing the building block patterns.

highlight.name: 1d9ed5ef-d36c-b85b-59de-009d6c03b917
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: 1. A Tracking Query that can access past and present handling of a particular Cargo 2. A Booking Application that allows a new Cargo to be registered and prepares the system for it 3. An Incident Logging Application that can record each handling of the Cargo (providing the information that is found by the Tracking Query)

highlight.name: f8080b35-c4a5-9830-94d2-184af43c612f
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: , an entity in the usual sense of the word

highlight.name: dceed966-1cae-c899-3d9d-d7d34deb7a13
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: This question calls for consultation with a domain expert.

highlight.name: 0ddb3306-0815-29ac-f7ff-c2e94e435f2c
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It will initially be a manual entry.

highlight.name: d0e17297-831f-4928-4478-06c30570cccf
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: crates

highlight.name: 1103392d-9d12-36ad-4f7d-7944522bf723
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Two places with the same name are not the same. Latitude and longitude could provide a unique key, but probably not a very practical one, since those measurements are not of interest to most purposes of this system, and they would be fairly complicated.

highlight.name: 77ae7015-c499-9686-854e-0e4997088ad5
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: More likely, the Location will be part of a geographical model of some kind that will relate places according to shipping lanes and other domain-specific concerns. So an arbitrary, internal, automatically generated identifier will suffice.

highlight.name: d8c55bef-2095-c779-5485-c807e907622e
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: If the Customer has a direct reference to every Cargo it has shipped, it will become cumbersome for long- term, repeat Customers.

highlight.name: 0adf3c4f-6671-f4db-449a-97b2423228e2
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: If our application were tracking the inventory of ships, traversal from Carrier Movement to Handling Event would be important. But our business needs to track only the Cargo. Making the association traversable only from Handling Event to Carrier Movement captures that understanding of our business. This also reduces the implementation to a simple object reference, because the direction with multiplicity was disallowed.

highlight.name: 41fd0f59-e94d-f4ce-a7ec-77bdcf56a58b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: There is one circular reference in our model: Cargo knows its Delivery History, which holds a series of Handling Events, which in turn point back to the Cargo.

highlight.name: 5636a311-1b5f-ca1c-23f7-e42b1fd732ae
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Circular references logically exist in many domains and are sometimes necessary in design as well, but they are tricky to maintain. Implementation choices can help by avoiding holding the same information in two places that must be kept synchronized. In this case, we can make a simple but fragile implementation (in Java) in an initial prototype, by giving Delivery History a List object containing Handling Events. But at some point we’ll probably want to drop the collection in favor of a database lookup with Cargo as the key. This discussion will be taken up again when choosing REPOSITORIES. If the query to see the history is relatively infrequent, this should give good performance, simplify maintenance, and reduce the overhead of adding Handling Events. If this query is very frequent, then it is better to go ahead and maintain the direct pointer. These design trade-offs balance simplicity of implementation against performance. The model is the same; it contains the cycle and the bidirectional association.

highlight.name: 9f4236a6-25ff-6566-056c-00a37809ee17
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: one, to find the Handling Events for a Delivery History as a possible alternative to the collection, would be local within the Cargo AGGREGATE; the other would be used to find all the operations to load and prepare for a particular Carrier Movement.

highlight.name: 310f2e93-95c6-2572-5f59-f19f2841ab54
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: scratch.

highlight.name: a44ef1e2-bf5b-498a-829d-f8390ac04241
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: intact.

highlight.name: 20d7aefc-906a-48de-cb02-8bab4de91e3a
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Adding a Handling Event

highlight.name: ecfe26fb-1ad2-375e-b237-d5b4d080df8f
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: As discussed previously, the Handling Event is uniquely identified by the combination of the ID of its Cargo, the completion time, and the event type.

highlight.name: 835b66c8-df44-6fd0-90bc-935081826e09
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Unfortunately, the story isn’t quite that simple. The cycle of references, from Cargo to Delivery History to History Event and back to Cargo, complicates instance creation. The Delivery History holds a collection of Handling Events relevant to its Cargo, and the new object must be added to this collection as part of the transaction. If this back-pointer were not created, the objects would be inconsistent.

highlight.name: e67c1eab-de1d-79b0-bfcf-c8e29145f76b
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Modeling and design is not a constant forward process. It will grind to a halt unless there is frequent refactoring to take advantage of new insights to improve the model and the design.

highlight.name: ccabb556-2a40-c49c-9e48-1a156b0bf37d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Let’s go back to one of them and, with the benefit of hindsight, stack the design deck in our favor.

highlight.name: bce2b528-2bc5-9cf7-0654-9f2586938930
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The need to update Delivery History when adding a Handling Event gets the Cargo AGGREGATE involved in the transaction. If some other user was modifying Cargo at the same time, the Handling Event transaction could fail or be delayed. Entering a Handling Event is an operational activity that needs to be quick and simple, so an important application requirement is the ability to enter Handling Events without contention. This pushes us to consider a different design.

highlight.name: 9737e5bd-483a-4c37-2dfe-91ad7713859c
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: To take responsibility for the queries, we’ll add a REPOSITORY for Handling Events.

highlight.name: 6cc30e08-fe77-7424-73a2-b7ef0aaf56b9
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: incarnations.

highlight.name: ffe36700-dbca-abb8-4a0b-98b5cb41cdb4
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: If the common usage pattern is that the user seldom queries for the status of a Cargo until it arrives, then a lot of unneeded work will be avoided altogether.

highlight.name: 5c53effe-34ec-daf3-1479-f38c118e7ec1
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Replacing the Delivery History’s collection of Handling Events with a query would allow Handling Events to be added without raising any integrity issues outside its own AGGREGATE.

highlight.name: 6f245c58-c4df-f001-ad4a-c3b8567d4e55
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Using a query rather than a collection would also reduce the difficulty of maintaining consistency in the cyclical reference between Cargo and Handling Event.

highlight.name: c5078183-e74e-d553-3674-713f979393f4
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The result is that objects that conceptually have little relationship (low cohesion) are crammed together, and associations run willy-nilly between all the MODULES (high coupling).

highlight.name: f4a3f84d-37ee-8ebb-2ab9-509e27eb5381
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: Partitioning by pattern may seem like an obvious error, but it is not really any less sensible than separating persistent objects from transient ones or any other methodical scheme that is not grounded in the meaning of the objects.

highlight.name: 8602a2f6-12b5-6411-5607-c78756be17f6
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The back office takes care of billing, submitting invoices according to the pricing in the customer’s agreement.

highlight.name: f28b51af-1d2a-0b64-c7af-97ac16948b86
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: This intuitive breakdown could be refined, certainly, in successive iterations, or even replaced entirely, but it is now aiding MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN and contributing to the UBIQUITOUS LANGUAGE.

highlight.name: 59a35810-1635-220f-f47e-6663e3e8fc91
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: hypothetical

highlight.name: bddcdabf-17db-5dfd-5e55-59050a0363d4
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: This diagram is a variation on the infrastructure-driven packaging problem raised in Chapter 5.

highlight.name: 56a908d6-9bc1-3e78-721d-89e5a38cc723
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: One feature supports yield management by allowing the firm to allocate how much cargo of specific types they will attempt to book based on the type of goods, the origin and destination, or any other factor they may choose that can be entered as a category name.

highlight.name: cebe42c7-f98f-13ae-ffe6-7aeefe25be8e
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: imaginary shipping company

highlight.name: 41570581-ced0-312c-7391-b3a73efa419c
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The sales division

highlight.name: 8eb2c247-2f26-0ce4-5709-35cc0fc538b2
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: be crowded out

highlight.name: 9afc9242-d671-7bdc-fbcc-bb4a18f86382
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: If the Booking Application interacts with it directly, our application will have to accommodate the other system’s design, which will make it harder to keep a clear MODEL-DRIVEN DESIGN and will confuse the UBIQUITOUS LANGUAGE.

highlight.name: ce91f261-c5f0-967d-d0b5-dd02c1b15095
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It will not be a general translation mechanism. It will expose just the features our application needs, and it will reabstract them in terms of our domain model.

highlight.name: d3b962a5-2587-65c9-f13b-5fe825d61c8e
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: But we would be missing an opportunity to use language to recast the problem along lines more useful to us.

highlight.name: 2888fc44-6add-9d49-1d2c-961bd3b4314a
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The book Analysis Patterns (Fowler 1996) describes a pattern that addresses this kind of problem: the ENTERPRISE SEGMENT.

highlight.name: b2183ce2-6433-cc1b-5244-b32b95fbfc4d
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The Allocation Checker will translate between Enterprise Segments and the category names of the external system.

highlight.name: ef340322-051e-89e3-1dd2-717097c23c10
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: It isn’t clear how the Booking Application derives the Enterprise Segment.

highlight.name: afd3acc9-192c-d725-5fc7-a4605155d2b3
highlight.title: Guest
highlight.subject: 高亮
highlight.contents: The only serious constraint imposed by this integration will be that the Sales Management System mustn’t use dimensions that the Allocation Checker can’t turn into Enterprise Segments.
